messages.text:
### GitHub issue #202 (workflow:architecture)
**Generate technical architecture from features board**

## Context

Board seeding completed (see issue #<SEED>). Produce or refine technical architecture from the features board and project documentation.
 
## Requirements

- Use `boards/features.md`, `docs/features/`, and `docs/project/` as inputs
- Follow bootstrap / architecture prompts for your repo
- Align output with `boards/technical-architecture.md` expectations

## Source

This issue was manually created to start the architecture stage (after board seeding issue #<SEED>, or without a seed issue if skipped).

<!-- seed-br-features-source-issue: #<SEED> -->

---

# Architecture: Feature to Technical Architecture

You are running in the **target repository**. Do not assume prompts live in an orchestrator codebase.

## Instructions

1. **Architecture prompts**: Look for and read these in this repo (they may be under `.cursor/prompts/` or `docs/.cursor/prompts/`):
   - `github-issue-feature-to-architecture.md`
   - `feature-breakdown-to-architecture.md`
   - `review-pass.md`
   - `board-update.md`
2. **Supporting docs** (read when present; **create boards if missing**):
   - `boards/features.md` — if missing, **create** a minimal features board (table scaffold + rows from issue context and `docs/features/` as needed). Do **not** stop or add `needs-info` only because this file is absent.
   - `boards/technical-architecture.md` — if missing, **create** a minimal architecture board (table scaffold). Do **not** stop or add `needs-info` only because this file is absent.
   - `docs/project/architecture-overview.md` and `docs/project/standards.md` — use as source of truth when present; if missing, infer from other `docs/project/` files and state assumptions in the commit message.
   - `docs/project/domain-model.md`, `api-standards.md`, `data-standards.md` — optional when present.
   Only use `needs-info` for **genuine ambiguities** (e.g. issue body gives no scope and no paths to read), not for absent board files.

3. **Resolve the target feature**:
   - Read the issue body and title to identify the architecture item (e.g., ARCH-003) or feature (e.g., FEAT-005)
   - Use `boards/features.md` when it exists; if you just created it, add the relevant feature row(s) as part of this run
   - Identify the source feature spec from the feature board, `docs/features/`, or issue body

4. **Generate architecture artifacts** under `docs/project/architecture/`:
   - **One markdown file per architecture item (ARCH-###)** that needs a full write-up. Use stable names like `docs/project/architecture/ARCH-001-short-slug.md` (slug from feature name). Each file is the **canonical** deep spec for that ARCH row: components, data flow, APIs/contracts, integrations, risks, NFRs, readiness criteria, open decisions — not a summary paragraph.
   - **Portfolio / batch issues** (e.g. "feature portfolio", component map covering ARCH-001…ARCH-014): keep a **single** umbrella doc (e.g. `docs/project/architecture/architecture-portfolio-overview.md` or the issue-driven title) **high level only** — shared planes, portfolio risks, and the **architecture component map table**. Do **not** paste full per-ARCH narratives into that umbrella doc; **link** to each `ARCH-###-*.md` instead.
   - If an ARCH row exists only in the map table, still add/refresh its **dedicated** file with full detail (or explicitly mark TBD in that file with a short reason — avoid leaving detail only in the umbrella doc).
   - Add or update `docs/project/architecture/README.md` as an index listing ARCH ID → file path when multiple files exist.
   - Follow `feature-breakdown-to-architecture.md` for section structure **inside each per-ARCH file**.

5. **Update the architecture board**:
   - Update `boards/technical-architecture.md` for this issue
   - Set status appropriately (e.g., Pushed or Done when branch is pushed; do **not** use In PR — no pull requests)
   - **Output** column: point each ARCH row to its **canonical** `docs/project/architecture/ARCH-###-*.md` path (not only the umbrella portfolio doc)

6. **Complete the run — do not stop after planning**:
   - Do **not** end after "Environment ready", gap analysis, or "Next I will…" without writing files. In one run: create **all** required `ARCH-###-*.md` files, trim umbrella to map+links, update `docs/project/architecture/README.md`, rewire board **Output** columns, then **commit and push**.
   - If length is a concern: ship concise but complete per-ARCH files first, then push — do not defer the whole batch to a hypothetical follow-up.

7. **Commit and push**:
   - Use branch `arch/issue-202` (e.g., `arch/issue-202` or `arch/feat-005-recommendation-delivery-api`)
   - Commit all changes
   - Push to origin
   - Changes will be committed directly to `main`

8. **Do NOT** create:
   - implementation-plan rows
   - implementation-plan issues
   - UI/backend/integration/QA issues

---

## Issue title

Generate technical architecture from features board

## Issue body

## Context

Board seeding completed (see issue #<SEED>). Produce or refine technical architecture from the features board and project documentation.
 
## Requirements

- Use `boards/features.md`, `docs/features/`, and `docs/project/` as inputs
- Follow bootstrap / architecture prompts for your repo
- Align output with `boards/technical-architecture.md` expectations

## Source

This issue was manually created to start the architecture stage (after board seeding issue #<SEED>, or without a seed issue if skipped).

<!-- seed-br-features-source-issue: #<SEED> -->

## Board path

boards/technical-architecture.md

I’ll generate the architecture artifacts end-to-end from the existing features and project docs, then update the architecture board accordingly. First I’m locating the required prompt files and current board/docs state so I can map features to ARCH items before editing.

I found all required prompt files plus existing `boards/features.md`, `boards/technical-architecture.md`, and project standards docs; next I’m reading them to map each feature to ARCH outputs and determine what needs to be created or refined.

I’ve confirmed the board already has ARCH-001 through ARCH-014 but all rows still point to shared umbrella docs, which violates the per-ARCH canonical artifact requirement. I’m now creating 14 dedicated `ARCH-###-*.md` specs, then I’ll trim the umbrella doc to map + links, add an architecture README index, and rewire `boards/technical-architecture.md` outputs to the canonical per-ARCH files before committing and pushing.
